rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Plaintiffs Claire And Martin Ryans Motions To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 7, 2020 line 1 . PLAINTIFF MARTIN RYAN, CLAIRE RYAN Amended Motions To Compel Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel. Hearing Required. Parties are ordered to engage in good faith meet and confer efforts no later than 8:30 a.m. on August 7, 2020 and to report their progress to the Court at 9:30 a.m. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ARTI KUTHIALA ET AL VS. GINA VELEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Dante Cecchini And Cecchini Realty Co.'S Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 7, 2020 line 4. Defendants Dante Cecchini And Cecchini Realty Co.'S Motion For Change Of Venue Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Cecchini's Motion to Change Venue is DENIED. Russello v. Mori (1957) 153 Cal.App.2d 828; ?De Long v. De Long (1954) 127 Cal.App.2d 373. Request for fees is denied. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ARTI KUTHIALA ET AL VS. GINA VELEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Gina Velez, Individually And As Trustess Of The Gina J. Velez Trust'S Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 7, 2020 line 4. Defendant Gina Velez, Individually And As Trustess Of The Gina J. Velez Trust'S Motion To Change Venue Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Velez' Motion to Change Venue. is DENIED. The motion is made on the grounds that "San Francisco is an improper venue." (Notice of Motion 2:3). Defendant appears CCP 395(a) "If the action is for injury to person , the superior court in either the county where the injury occurs or the county where the defendants, or some of them reside at the commencement of the action, is a proper court for the trial of the action." There is no evidence provided by Defendant that San Francisco was an improper venue at the time of the filing of the Complaint. Defendant appears to suggest that San Mateo is "more appropriate" and "makes more sense" in the Points and Authorities, but these grounds are not listed in the Notice of Motion. Request for fees is denied. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 7, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT DAVID LOWE DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Acts required to be performed by an owner to initiate and maintain an Ellis Act procedure have not been performed by the such owner. Cases cited by the Plaintiff are not on point. = (501/CFH)
